repomix_thesis.name: Final_Thesis_Academix_v13_2
repomix_thesis.summary: Complete thesis text for Academix v13.2 -- a pure VBA Decision Support System (نظام دعم قرار) for stock management at the Directorate of Education, El Bayadh, Algeria. 4 chapters aligned with CNEPD BTS standards.

structure.chapter: Theoretical framework covering logistics importance, stock management theory, purchase function, documentary cycle, accounting valuation methods (CMUP, FIFO, LIFO), supplier evaluation, and ERP concepts. Establishes the DSS narrative: Excel/VBA as Decision Support System for zero-cost digitization.

Geographic/institutional framework of El Bayadh, advanced mathematical models (Wilson EOQ, ABC/XYZ analysis), field diagnosis of structural weaknesses (phantom stock, no automated alerts, duplicate registration), and quantitative field data (85 days observation).

DSS architecture design -- why pure VBA, dual-guard system, hexagonal guard chain, local digital ledger, VBA engine with 4 core functions (stock validation, CMUP recalculation, ROP evaluation, atomic writing), Bento Grid UI design, and 20 functional features.

Quantitative verification of hypotheses (ROP alert regularity 0% to 100%, elimination of phantom stocks), Toner G030 case study (D=1546, ROP=205.6, SS=200, Q*=176, LT=2), performance evaluation, administrative impact, and future development prospects.

key_passages.passage: Opening: The thesis bridges CNEPD logistics theory with field reality at El Bayadh Directorate of Education. Digital transformation is not replacing paper with software, but re-engineering resource management.

Phantom Stock phenomenon: Discrepancy between book balance and physical stock. In El Bayadh warehouse, 236 unit difference found for A4 paper reams alone. Causes: registration errors, delayed updates, double/incomplete entries.
Stockout: 3 documented stockout cases for Class A critical items during 38 observation days, all due to absence of automated alert mechanisms.

Documentary cycle: Demande d'Achat → Bon de Commande → Bon de Réception → Bon de Sortie → Fiche de Stock. Field finding: stock cards systematically lack reference document numbers, nullifying audit value.
CMUP formula: CMUP_جديد = [(Q_قائم × CMUP_سابق) + (Q_جديدة × PU_جديدة)] ÷ (Q_قائم + Q_جديدة)
EOQ formula: Q* = √(2 × D × CL) ÷ (t × PU). ROP formula: ROP = SS + (D ÷ 250) × LT
ABC-XYZ matrix: A (~20% items, ~80% value), B (~30%, ~15%), C (~50%, ~5%). XYZ by CoV: X (CoV<0.25 regular), Y (0.25-0.50 variable), Z (≥0.50 random). ART-001 classified AX, ART-005 classified AY.

7 structural weaknesses: (1) No automated alerts → 3 stockout cases, (2) Duplicate registration → contradictions, (3) Phantom stocks → 236 unit gap, (4) Long inventory time → full day for manual matching, (5) No historical tracking → zero traceability, (6) No supplier evaluation → arbitrary selection, (7) Missing reference numbers → empty audit value.

Hexagonal guard chain: (1) Field completion, (2) Document reference mandatory, (3) Quantity validity (qty>0, integer), (4) Negative stock prevention, (5) Date format validation (DD/MM/YYYY), (6) French comma-to-dot conversion.

Engine operation sequence: (1) Stock validation for every OUT request → HTTP 409 rejection, (2) CMUP recalculation for every IN request, (3) ROP evaluation after every successful movement, (4) Atomic writing after stages 1-3 succeed → no partial writes.
Dual-Guard System: Level 1 (VBA UI) checks available stock before exit, Level 2 (VBA engine) re-checks before writing. Before: 236 unit gap. After: 100% match between accounting and physical stock.
ROP classification: NORMAL (green) stock>ROP, WARNING (amber) SS<stock≤ROP (issue purchase order), CRITICAL (red) stock≤SS (immediate intervention).
Hypothesis 1: ROP alert regularity KPI-04. Before: 0% (no automated alerts). After: 100% regularity. 55 days continuous field testing.

Hypothesis 2: Elimination of phantom stocks. Dual-guard: Front guard (VBA layer) via SUMIFS before any exit, back guard (VBA engine) re-checks before database write. Result: 100% match between accounting and physical stock for the first time.

Toner G030 case study: D=1,546, SS=200, PU=700 DZD, LT=2 days. Q*=176, ROP=205.6. System maintained safe stock above ROP at 92%. Zero stockouts during 55-day application vs 3 stockouts in 38 days before.
Time efficiency: Manual system took 20-30 minutes per operation. With DSS hybrid architecture (VBA + processing engine), processing reduced to seconds.

General conclusion: Digital transformation bridges CNEPD theory with El Bayadh field reality. D=1,546, Q*=176, ROP=205.6, SS=200 achieved 100% perpetual inventory accuracy, processing time reduced from 30 minutes to seconds. Smart low-cost solutions achieve strategic results.

references.ref: Ballou, R.H. (2004). Business Logistics/Supply Chain Management
Chopra, S. & Meindl, P. (2016). Supply Chain Management
Richards, G. & Grinsted, S. (2024). The Routledge Companion to Logistics
Waters, D. (2022). Inventory Management and Control

appendices.appendix: System file structure -- Excel sheet layout (dashboard, ARTICLES, MOUVEMENTS, ANALYSE)
Main VBA modules (mod_StockEngine, mod_Dashboard, mod_ExportEngine, mod_LocalSync, mod_AuditTrail)
Field stock data sample (ART-001 A4 Paper: stock 289, ROP 205.6, class AX; ART-005 Toner G030: stock 1, ROP 205.6, class CX)
Generated documents: BR, BS, DA templates
